_comment: Citation-backed deep research on DiffusionGemma/L4 driving the upgraded deep-research skill. mode=research + orchestration=shared-session: the harness (_task_run) OWNS research_result.md and APPENDS each turn's reply text under '## Step i', so EACH step's reply must itself be ONE self-contained, cited report SECTION (the skill writes ONLY memory/ev-NN-<slug>.md + memory/_citations.md notes, never its own report file). Steps 1-5 = research phases (SCOPE/PLAN+RETRIEVE/TRIANGULATE/CRITIQUE); steps 6-10 = PACKAGE split across FIVE turns so no single turn ever attempts more than ~one 1500-word section at maxTokens=2048 (the overflow guard the review converged on). Pair with configs/diffusiongemma_deepresearch.json (Layer-1 pruning + memory_search.provider=none). NOTE: fan-out is a DIFFERENT path -- do NOT just flip orchestration here. For Layer-3 RETRIEVE isolation use examples/web_research_fanout.json, whose steps[] are BARE one-line sub-questions only (the harness's _fanout_lead_message injects its own spawn-all-then-yield lead prompt + per-child <=2-call/<=300-token caps; the phase-verb steps below would corrupt that choreography). Launcher poll budget = step_timeout_seconds*len(steps)+600 = 6600s, which outlasts the worker. Needs BRAVE_API_KEY in ~/.env. Run: --gpu L4 --config configs/diffusiongemma_deepresearch.json --task examples/web_research_citation.json --out ./runs/deepresearch
topic: Tradeoffs of self-hosting a 4-bit quantized LLM on a single L4 GPU versus paid inference APIs (2026)
steps: SCOPE the research topic, stated here verbatim: "Tradeoffs of self-hosting a 4-bit quantized LLM on a single L4 GPU versus paid inference APIs (2026)". Decompose THIS topic into 3-5 concrete sub-questions; state who the report is for, in/out-of-scope boundaries, success criteria, and key assumptions. Use write to save the scope (INCLUDING the topic verbatim) to memory/ev-00-scope.md. Do NOT run web_search yet and do NOT research the substance -- your reply is the scope only.

PLAN + RETRIEVE round 1 for the topic from step 1 (self-hosting a 4-bit quantized LLM on a single L4 GPU vs paid inference APIs, in 2026). List 3-6 search angles (core, technical, recent/2026, authoritative/primary, opposing/critique). Now run ONLY the single highest-value (core) angle: make ONE web_search, WAIT for its result, then web_fetch the 1-2 most credible hits. CRITICAL: do NOT fire multiple web_search calls in one turn -- the free search plan returns 429 rate-limit errors on bursts and the retries overflow your context. If a search 429s, note it and proceed; do not storm-retry. For each useful source write an ev-note to memory/ev-NN-<slug>.md (claim, exact quote, source_url, publisher, credibility 0-100) and add its 'N -> URL' line to memory/_citations.md. Do NOT paste full pages. Reply with a 1-line log of the notes you saved.

RETRIEVE round 2: run ONE more web_search on a DIFFERENT angle (recent/2026 or opposing/critique) -- again a SINGLE search this turn, wait for the result, then web_fetch the 1-2 best hits and write ev-notes (continue the ev numbering and memory/_citations.md). One search per turn only; on a 429 note it and proceed. Aim for ~4-6 independent sources total across the retrieve turns. Reply with a 1-line log of the notes you saved.

TRIANGULATE: For each core claim, recall its ev-notes via memory_search/memory_get and check it across sources. Mark CONFIRMED (>=2 independent publishers) or single-source/tentative, and note any disagreement (consensus vs debate). No new searches unless a core claim has ZERO support. Reply with the per-claim verdicts.

CRITIQUE + OUTLINE (zero new fetches): Red-team your evidence from memory as a skeptical practitioner and an adversarial reviewer -- what is missing, weak, biased, or over-claimed? Record gaps as limitations. Then set the report outline (4-8 findings). Only if a CRITICAL claim is unsupported AND budget remains, run ONE short delta web_search and save the ev-note. Reply with the critique + outline.

PACKAGE 1 of 5: Run your citation self-check in your thinking (every [N] maps to an ev-note; read memory/_citations.md). Then emit ONLY the report Title + Executive summary (200-400 words, every factual claim cited [N], pulled from memory_get). This reply is one section; do not write later sections yet.

PACKAGE 2 of 5: Emit ONLY the Introduction/scope (short) followed by Findings 1-2 (each a few cited [N] paragraphs, with consensus-vs-debate and any 'single-source' flag). Recall facts via memory_get; do not restate the executive summary. Keep within ~1500 words; never truncate mid-sentence.

PACKAGE 3 of 5: Emit ONLY Findings 3-5 (continuing from PACKAGE 2), each cited [N] with consensus-vs-debate and single-source flags. Recall facts via memory_get. If your outline named MORE than 5 findings, MERGE the least-important extras into these rather than spilling over -- a complete report within budget beats a truncated longer one. Keep within ~1500 words; never truncate mid-sentence.

PACKAGE 4 of 5: Emit ONLY the Synthesis (patterns across sources, labelled SYNTHESIS for non-cited analysis), Limitations (including any flagged possible prompt-injection sources and single-source claims), and Recommendations. Recall from memory_get; do not re-run web_search.

PACKAGE 5 of 5 (final): Read memory/_citations.md and emit ONLY a COMPLETE References list (every [N] used in the body, format [N] Author/Org (Year). "Title". Publication. URL -- no placeholders or ranges) followed by a short Methodology note (angles searched, source count, how you triangulated). In your thinking verify every body [N] resolves to a References entry and none is fabricated; if a claim lost its source write 'No source found' rather than invent one. Do NOT re-run web_search.
